meta.purpose: Scientific evidence for Add Good ingredient pages. Each ingredient has multiple peer-reviewed studies with DOI links. Use for 'Science behind' sections on product pages and blog content.
meta.note_on_fermentation: Fermentation is referenced only as an ingredient-level property (Rice Ferment, Sea Kelp Bioferment), NOT as a brand identity. Add Good's scalable brand frame is 'plant & fungi science' or 'nature, enhanced'.
meta.changelog_v2: Updated to v5 ingredient architecture. Removed: bakuchiol (oil-soluble), squalane (oil), astaxanthin/Red Gold (oil-soluble, no topical RCTs, indefensible claims). Added: GHK-Cu, Beta-Glucan, PGA. Updated: licorice (messaging guardrails applied — no brightening/lightening/whitening; shifted from glabridin to water-soluble actives). Updated: peptide_complex display name to Matrixyl 3000.

niacinamide.summary: One of the most extensively studied actives in dermatology. A water-soluble form of vitamin B3 that improves skin tone, reduces hyperpigmentation, strengthens the barrier, and controls sebum — with an excellent safety profile across all skin types.
key_claims: Reduces hyperpigmentation by inhibiting melanosome transfer
Strengthens skin barrier by boosting ceramide and fatty acid synthesis
Clinically proven to improve fine lines, texture, and skin tone
Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties
Controls sebum production

studies.title: Niacinamide efficacy in skin therapy: A comprehensive literature review
studies.finding: Niacinamide is effective across nearly all skin conditions including aging, hyperpigmentation, acne, and barrier dysfunction. Topical application boosts ceramide, fatty acid, and cholesterol synthesis in keratinocytes, confirmed by multiple RCTs.

studies.title: Exploring Niacinamide as a Multifunctional Agent for Skin Health and Rejuvenation
studies.finding: Clinical studies affirm niacinamide's effectiveness in enhancing skin tone, texture, and barrier integrity, with minimal adverse effects even with prolonged use.

studies.title: Pilot Study: Cosmetic Serum Containing Niacinamide, Postbiotics and Peptides — A Randomized Controlled Trial
studies.finding: Significant improvement in skin hydration observed at week 4 (61.0 vs 68.6 AU, p<0.05). Niacinamide accelerates keratinocyte differentiation and prevents oxidative senescence.

studies.title: Cosmeceutical Aptitudes of Niacinamide: A Review
studies.finding: Niacinamide possesses anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antipruritic properties. Verified effective for aging, hyperpigmentation, acne, psoriasis, dermatitis, and more.

studies.title: Topical Niacinamide in Daily Skincare: A 3-Week Real-World Study
studies.finding: Niacinamide preparation was significantly more effective in reducing visible facial spots and pigmentation appearance compared to ceramide-only and usual skincare groups.

rice_ferment.summary: Fermentation of rice by Saccharomyces yeast produces a filtrate rich in amino acids, peptides, and organic acids that weren't present in the raw grain. Used for centuries in Asian skincare traditions, now backed by modern research on skin tone improvement and barrier support.
key_claims: Rich in amino acids, vitamins, minerals, and organic acids
Improves skin tone and evens out complexion
Fermentation increases bioavailability of active compounds
Strengthens skin barrier function
Antioxidant protection

studies.title: Effect of fermented rice water extract on the dermal thickness and collagen density
studies.finding: Fermented rice water increased dermal collagen density and skin thickness, supporting its traditional use for skin tone improvement and anti-aging.

studies.title: Saccharomyces ferment filtrate: composition and cosmetic benefits
studies.finding: Saccharomyces fermentation produces bioactive metabolites including amino acids, beta-glucans, and vitamins that improve skin hydration, radiance, and barrier function.

studies.title: Rice bran fermented extract: antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects on skin
studies.finding: Fermented rice bran extract showed significant antioxidant activity and reduced UV-induced skin inflammation in animal models.

tremella.summary: An edible fungus prized in Chinese medicine for centuries. Its polysaccharides hold up to 500× their weight in water — with smaller molecular size than many forms of hyaluronic acid, enabling deeper penetration into the skin.
key_claims: Holds up to 500× its weight in water
Smaller molecules than hyaluronic acid — deeper skin penetration
Anti-aging and photoprotection properties
Protects skin fibroblasts from oxidative stress
Improves skin texture, reduces roughness, increases flexibility

studies.title: The potential cutaneous benefits of Tremella fuciformis
studies.finding: Tremella fuciformis extract demonstrated roles in skin anti-aging, photoprotection, wound healing, and barrier protection across multiple studies.

studies.title: Tremella fuciformis polysaccharide suppresses hydrogen peroxide-triggered injury of human skin fibroblasts via upregulation of SIRT1
studies.finding: TFPS at 100–200 µg/ml significantly reduced oxidative stress-induced injury in human skin fibroblasts, suppressed ROS production and apoptosis through SIRT1 pathway activation.

studies.title: Effect of polysaccharides from Tremella fuciformis on UV-induced photoaging
studies.finding: Tremella polysaccharides efficiently reduced UV-induced water and collagen loss in skin, inhibited glycosaminoglycan increase, and showed excellent moisturizing and anti-wrinkle effects.

studies.title: Structure characterization and moisturizing effect of Tremella polysaccharide
studies.finding: Fermented Tremella polysaccharide significantly increased epidermal moisture content in a dose-dependent manner and promoted expression of moisturizing genes (AQP3, FLG, HYAL2) in human keratinocytes.

studies.title: A review on the production, structure, bioactivities and applications of Tremella polysaccharides
studies.finding: Decades of studies demonstrate Tremella polysaccharide's potent moisturizing, anti-inflammatory, wound healing, anti-oxidation, anti-aging, and anti-radiation effects with excellent safety.

hyaluronic_acid.summary: A naturally occurring polysaccharide that holds up to 1000× its weight in water. Multi-molecular weight formulations deliver hydration at every skin level — high MW forms a protective film on the surface, while low MW penetrates deeper for lasting plumping.
key_claims: Holds up to 1000× its weight in water
Multi-molecular weight ensures hydration at every skin layer
Clinically proven to reduce wrinkle depth and improve skin elasticity
Biocompatible — naturally present in human skin

studies.title: Efficacy of cream-based novel formulations of hyaluronic acid of different molecular weights in anti-wrinkle treatment
studies.finding: Multi-molecular HA formulation significantly reduced wrinkle depth (up to 40%) and improved skin hydration and elasticity after 60 days of treatment.

studies.title: Hyaluronic acid: A key molecule in skin aging
studies.finding: HA plays a critical role in skin moisture, tissue repair, and structural integrity. Its depletion is a major factor in skin aging.

sea_kelp_bioferment.summary: Brown kelp from cold Atlantic currents, fermented with Lactobacillus probiotics. Fermentation breaks open algae cells, releasing minerals, vitamins, and amino acids in a bioavailable form the skin readily accepts.
key_claims: Rich in minerals (iodine, calcium, magnesium, zinc, iron)
Fermentation increases bioavailability of active compounds
Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties
Supports collagen synthesis and skin firmness

studies.title: Brown seaweed polysaccharides: biological properties and potential applications in dermatology
studies.finding: Brown seaweed polysaccharides (fucoidans, alginates, laminarins) exhibit anti-aging, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound healing properties relevant to dermatological applications.

studies.title: Marine bioactives as functional ingredients for cosmeceuticals
studies.finding: Marine-derived bioactives including kelp extracts demonstrate significant photoprotective, anti-wrinkle, and moisturizing effects through multiple mechanisms.

studies.title: Lactobacillus fermentation enhances bioactivity of marine polysaccharides
studies.finding: Probiotic fermentation of seaweed polysaccharides significantly increased antioxidant activity and improved bioavailability of minerals and amino acids compared to non-fermented extracts.

matrixyl_3000.summary: A patented signal peptide complex that communicates with skin cells to stimulate collagen production and reduce inflammation. Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1 mimics the body's own collagen-building signals, while Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7 suppresses inflammatory mediators. Arrives pre-solubilized in glycerin/water/butylene glycol — fully gel-compatible.
key_claims: Stimulates collagen I and III synthesis
Reduces appearance of wrinkles — up to 45% reduction in wrinkle area in clinical studies
Anti-inflammatory — reduces IL-6 production
Clinically proven efficacy in controlled studies
Pre-solubilized — fully compatible with aqueous gel format

studies.title: Effect of a cosmetic product containing Palmitoyl Oligopeptide and Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7 on wrinkles
studies.finding: Significant reduction in wrinkle depth and volume after 2 months of twice-daily application. Improvements continued through 6 months with measurable increase in skin firmness and elasticity.

studies.title: Topical peptides as cosmeceuticals
studies.finding: Signal peptides including palmitoyl tripeptide-1 and tetrapeptide-7 demonstrate collagen-stimulating and anti-inflammatory effects, with growing clinical evidence supporting their anti-aging claims.

centella.summary: One of the most well-documented botanical ingredients in dermatology. Its triterpenoids (madecassoside, asiaticoside) stimulate collagen synthesis, suppress inflammation, and accelerate wound healing — validated by thousands of years of Ayurvedic use and modern clinical trials.
key_claims: Stimulates collagen I synthesis and fibroblast growth factor (FGF)
Reduces inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, IL-6, TNFα)
Accelerates wound healing and scar improvement
Strengthens skin barrier
Safe for sensitive and reactive skin

studies.title: Centella asiatica in dermatology: An overview of its benefits
studies.finding: Stimulates collagen I, FGF, and VEGF production. Reduces IL-1β, IL-6, and TNFα. Multiple RCTs confirm wound healing and scar improvement efficacy.

studies.title: Asiaticoside and madecassoside stimulate collagen synthesis
studies.finding: Madecassoside from Centella significantly stimulates type I collagen synthesis in human dermal fibroblasts and promotes wound healing through TGF-β signaling pathway.

studies.title: Centella asiatica: phytochemistry and mechanisms of wound healing
studies.finding: Comprehensive evidence supports Centella's wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and collagen-stimulating effects. Triterpenoid saponins are the primary active compounds.

ghkcu.summary: A naturally occurring tripeptide (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine) complexed with copper, present in human plasma, saliva, and skin. Levels decline by more than half after age 60 — coinciding with the decrease in skin's regenerative capacity. Over 20 years of published research demonstrate its ability to stimulate collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycan synthesis, improve skin density and thickness, and accelerate wound healing. 100% water-soluble.
key_claims: Naturally present in the human body — levels decline >50% after age 60
Stimulates synthesis of collagen, elastin, proteoglycans, and glycosaminoglycans
Clinically proven to increase skin density, thickness, and firmness
Outperformed vitamin C and retinoic acid in collagen production (clinical comparison)
Accelerates wound healing and tissue remodeling
Water-soluble — fully compatible with aqueous gel format

studies.title: Skin Care Benefits of Copper Peptide Containing Facial Cream
studies.authors: Leyden J, Stephens T, Finkey MB, Appa Y, Barkovic S
studies.finding: GHK-Cu facial cream applied for 12 weeks to 71 women with mild to advanced photoaging increased skin density and thickness, reduced laxity, improved clarity, and reduced fine lines and wrinkle depth.

studies.title: GHK-Cu eye cream study
studies.finding: GHK-Cu eye cream applied for 12 weeks to 41 women with mild to advanced photodamage outperformed both placebo and vitamin K cream in reducing lines and wrinkles, improving skin density, and increasing skin thickness.

studies.title: Effects of topical creams containing vitamin C, a copper-binding peptide cream and melatonin compared with tretinoin on the ultrastructure of normal skin
studies.authors: Abdulghani AA, Sherr S, Shirin S et al.
studies.finding: Topical GHK-Cu increased collagen production in 70% of treated subjects — outperforming both vitamin C (50%) and retinoic acid (40%).

studies.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data
studies.authors: Pickart L, Vasquez-Soltero JM, Margolina A
studies.finding: GHK-Cu regulates expression of multiple genes involved in skin repair, stimulates collagen and elastin synthesis, promotes angiogenesis, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant actions. Established safety record over decades of use.

studies.title: GHK Peptide as a Natural Modulator of Multiple Cellular Pathways in Skin Regeneration
studies.finding: GHK-Cu stimulates collagen synthesis, promotes fibroblast and keratinocyte growth, has antioxidant properties, and modulates metalloproteinase activity. GHK is naturally present in plasma at ~200 ng/mL at age 20 but declines to 80 ng/mL by age 60.

ghkcu.compatibility_note: GHK-Cu has a documented interaction with pure L-ascorbic acid. Most moisturizers use stable vitamin C derivatives, which are fully compatible. If the user's cream contains pure L-ascorbic acid, recommend applying the booster first, allowing 30 seconds absorption, then applying cream on top.

licorice.summary: One of the oldest medicinal plants in human history — used in Egypt, China, and Greece for millennia. Its water-soluble compounds, including glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, work through multiple pathways: calming inflammation via COX inhibition, protecting against UV-induced damage, and helping correct uneven skin tone through melanin dispersion. Gentle and effective without the side effects of synthetic depigmenting agents.
licorice.messaging_guardrail: NEVER use 'brightening', 'lightening', or 'whitening' in any customer-facing context. Safe alternatives: 'tone-evening', 'dark spot correcting', 'complexion-clarifying'. In aqueous extract, focus on water-soluble actives (liquiritin, glycyrrhizin), not glabridin (lipophilic — not present at meaningful levels in a water-based formula).
key_claims: Tone-evening — helps correct uneven complexion via melanin dispersion
Potent anti-inflammatory — glycyrrhizin as effective as 1% hydrocortisone in clinical comparison
Liquiritin protects against UVB-induced skin damage
Antioxidant protection through multiple pathways
Gentle — suitable for sensitive and reactive skin

studies.title: 18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: its core biological properties and dermatological applications
studies.finding: Glycyrrhizin and its metabolite glycyrrhetinic acid display potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Extensively documented for treatment of atopic dermatitis, UV-induced photoaging, and skin inflammation.

studies.title: The treatment of atopic dermatitis with licorice gel
studies.finding: 2% licorice gel (standardized for glycyrrhizinic acid) significantly reduced erythema, edema, and itching in atopic dermatitis patients versus vehicle, confirming licorice's clinical anti-inflammatory efficacy.

studies.title: Glycyrrhizinic acid and licorice extract display anti-inflammatory effects comparable to 1% hydrocortisone
studies.journal: PMC (New Herbal Biomedicines for Topical Treatment of Dermatological Disorders)
studies.finding: A composition with glycyrrhizinic acid (0.6%) and licorice extract as main actives was as effective as 1% hydrocortisone acetate in reducing UV-induced erythema in a placebo-controlled study.

studies.title: Liquiritin suppresses UVB-induced skin injury through prevention of inflammation, oxidative stress and apoptosis
studies.finding: Liquiritin (water-soluble flavonoid from licorice) significantly reduced UVB-induced inflammatory cytokines (IL-1β, TNF-α, IL-6, COX-2), oxidative stress, and apoptosis in skin cells and animal models.

studies.title: Topical liquiritin improves melasma
studies.finding: Topical liquiritin demonstrated clinical efficacy in improving melasma through melanin dispersion, providing a gentle approach to correcting uneven skin tone.

betaglucan.summary: A natural polysaccharide found in the cell walls of fungi, yeast, and grains. Fungi-derived beta-glucan activates the skin's own repair mechanisms — stimulating macrophages, enhancing barrier proteins, and promoting collagen deposition. Over 30 years of dermatological research. One of the fastest-rising skincare ingredients: search demand +181% year-over-year. 100% water-soluble, vegan, and fully compatible with aqueous gel format.
key_claims: Activates skin's innate repair — stimulates macrophages and barrier protein expression
Clinically demonstrated wound healing and barrier recovery
Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties
Deep skin penetration — reaches the dermis at just 0.1% concentration
Fungi-derived — aligns with 'plant & fungi science' brand frame
Search demand +181% year-over-year (Cult Beauty data, 2025)

studies.title: Exploring the Properties and Application Potential of β-Glucan in Skin Care
studies.finding: First systematic review of beta-glucan's clinical applications in dermatology. Confirms antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, barrier repair, and moisturizing effects. Demonstrated efficacy in wound healing, atopic dermatitis, UV protection, and photoaging.

studies.title: β-Glucans: Multi-Functional Modulator of Wound Healing
studies.finding: Beta-glucans enhance wound repair by increasing macrophage infiltration, stimulating tissue granulation, collagen deposition, and reepithelialization. Laboratory and animal studies provide compelling evidence for accelerated wound healing.

studies.title: Administration of skin care regimens containing β-glucan for skin recovery after fractional laser therapy
studies.finding: Beta-glucan-containing skincare regimen accelerated recovery of skin inflammation and barrier function after fractional laser therapy compared to vehicle control in a split-face clinical design.

studies.title: β-Glucan-Based Wet Dressing for Cutaneous Wound Healing
studies.finding: Beta-glucan hydrogel significantly accelerated wound healing and enhanced development of skin appendages. Increased expression of TGF-β3 indicated skin regeneration rather than scar-forming repair.

pga.summary: A biopolymer produced by Bacillus subtilis bacteria during fermentation of natto — a traditional Japanese fermented soybean. PGA holds 4,000–5,000× its weight in water (up to 5× more than hyaluronic acid). Its key mechanism: inhibiting hyaluronidase, the enzyme that breaks down the skin's own hyaluronic acid. Unlike ingredients that simply add hydration, PGA protects what the skin already has. 100% water-soluble, fermentation-derived.
key_claims: Holds 4,000–5,000× its weight in water — up to 5× more than hyaluronic acid
Inhibits hyaluronidase — protects skin's own hyaluronic acid from degradation
Boosts natural moisturizing factor (NMF) production on skin surface
Film-forming — creates protective microgel without requiring direct skin contact (works in mix-in format)
Fermentation-derived — from Bacillus subtilis via natto process

studies.title: In vitro evaluation of new functional properties of poly-γ-glutamic acid produced by Bacillus subtilis
studies.finding: PGA demonstrated stronger moisturizing effect than both collagen and hyaluronic acid. Different molecular weights of PGA showed effective inhibition of hyaluronidase activity in vitro.

studies.title: Gamma polyglutamic acid (gamma-PGA, H form), gamma-polyglutamate hydrogels for use as super moisturizers in cosmetic and personal care products
studies.finding: 0.2% PGA formulation increased skin hydration by 13.5% after 2 hours, compared to 7.5% for 5% glycerol. Confirmed superior moisturizing capacity to established humectants.

studies.title: Polyglutamate: Unleashing the Versatility of a Biopolymer for Cosmetic Industry Applications
studies.finding: PGA's biocompatibility, water solubility, and film-forming characteristics make it an ideal cosmetic ingredient. Applications include moisturizing, depigmentation, and skin barrier improvement. Fermentation-derived, sustainable production.

pga.format_note: PGA works via hydration and hyaluronidase inhibition — mechanisms that function in any format, including when blended with cream on the palm. This distinguishes it from pullulan (v4), whose film-forming tightening effect required direct skin contact and failed in the mix-in use case.
